seoTitle: Sunshine West vs Surf Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunshine West and Surf Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunshine West vs Surf Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunshine West and Surf Beach.
heading: Sunshine West vs Surf Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunshine West, Victoria and Surf Beach, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Surf Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Sunshine West (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunshine West or Surf Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunshine West has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Surf Beach scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunshine West scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Surf Beach scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunshine West or Surf Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Surf Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
